---
title: "PHYS 349 — Introduction to Quantum Computing"
subtitle: "Exam 1"
#author: "Dr. Jonathan Hood"
#date: today
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    header-includes: |
      \usepackage{enumitem}
      \usepackage{needspace}
      \newcommand{\ket}[1]{|#1\rangle}
      \newcommand{\bra}[1]{\langle#1|}
      \newcommand{\braket}[2]{\langle#1|#2\rangle}
      \newcommand{\ketbra}[2]{|#1\rangle\langle#2|}
---

\vspace{0.5cm}

**Name:** \hrulefill \hspace{2cm} **Date:** \hrulefill

\vspace{0.3cm}

**Instructions:** Select the best answer for each question. Circle your answer.

\vspace{0.5cm}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

\newpage

**1.** What is the result of applying the Pauli-$X$ gate to $\ket{0}$?

\begin{enumerate}[label=\Alph*)]
\item $\ket{0}$
\item $\ket{1}$
\item $\ket{+}$
\item $\ket{-}$
\item $i\ket{0}$
\end{enumerate}

\vspace{0.5cm}

\needspace{4cm}

**2.** Which of the following is a valid (normalized) single-qubit state?

\begin{enumerate}[label=\Alph*)]
\item $\frac{1}{2}\ket{0} + \frac{1}{2}\ket{1}$
\item $\ket{0} + \ket{1}$
\item $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\ket{0} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\ket{1}$
\item $2\ket{0} - \ket{1}$
\item $\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\ket{0} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\ket{1}$
\end{enumerate}

\vspace{0.5cm}

\needspace{4cm}

**3.** The eigenvalues of the Pauli-$Z$ operator are:

\begin{enumerate}[label=\Alph*)]
\item $0$ and $1$
\item $+1$ and $-1$
\item $+i$ and $-i$
\item $+\frac{1}{2}$ and $-\frac{1}{2}$
\item $0$ and $-1$
\end{enumerate}

\vspace{0.5cm}

\needspace{4cm}

**4.** What is the probability of measuring $\ket{0}$ for the state $\ket{+} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(\ket{0} + \ket{1})$?

\begin{enumerate}[label=\Alph*)]
\item $0$
\item $\frac{1}{4}$
\item $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
\item $\frac{1}{2}$
\item $1$
\end{enumerate}

\vspace{0.5cm}

\needspace{5cm}

**5.** Which matrix represents the Hadamard gate?

\begin{enumerate}[label=\Alph*)]
\item $\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{pmatrix}$
\item $\begin{pmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}$
\item $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{pmatrix}$
\item $\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{pmatrix}$
\item $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\begin{pmatrix} 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{pmatrix}$
\end{enumerate}
